text.title: Feature Flag Core Implementation Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the feature-flags-core-implementation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every flag is registered as a typed FeatureFlag dataclass with name + description + default + rollout_percent.
rule.rationale: Magic-string flag names drift; typed registration enables static analysis and audit.

rule.statement: All flag checks go through manager.is_enabled(name, user_id). Direct ENV / config reads scattered in code are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Multiple call paths bypass overrides, targeting, and audit; one call site keeps semantics consistent.

rule.statement: Env var (FF_*) overrides config file overrides default. Highest precedence wins.
rule.rationale: Operators need to override flags at runtime without redeploying config files.

rule.statement: Flag names are kebab-case (e.g. new-checkout-flow). FF_NEW_CHECKOUT_FLOW env maps to new-checkout-flow.
rule.rationale: Consistent naming maps cleanly between code, env, and dashboards.

rule.statement: Experiment-kind flags default OFF; ops/operational flags may default ON.
rule.rationale: Default OFF for new behavior prevents accidental release on first deploy.

rule.statement: is_enabled returns False for unknown flags. Never True-by-default for missing flags.
rule.rationale: Typos must fail closed, not open; True-by-default leaks new behavior to all users.

rule.statement: If using a managed flag SDK, or project has <3 flags ever, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Don't reimplement a managed SDK; trivial flag count does not justify a manager.
